articles.title: Ohio Casino Control Commission fines Kalshi prediction market
articles.summary: The Ohio Casino Control Commission slapped Kalshi with a fine for operating prediction markets in the state without proper licensing. This signals that state gaming regulators are starting to crack down on prediction market operators who blur the line between forecasting and gambling.

articles.title: WATCH LIVE: CFTC chairman testifies before House panel amid scrutiny of prediction markets
articles.summary: CFTC Chairman Rostin Behnam is testifying before Congress today as lawmakers grill him about the agency's oversight of prediction markets. The hearing signals growing political pressure on the CFTC to either crack down harder on platforms like Kalshi and PredictIt or clarify the murky regulatory landscape that's keeping bigger players on the sidelines.

articles.title: NFL Draft Prediction Markets Show Widespread Uncertainty Beyond No. 1
articles.summary: NFL draft prediction markets are showing clear consensus on the top pick while revealing massive uncertainty across the rest of the first round. This volatility creates prime opportunities for sharp bettors who can identify undervalued prospects before the market catches up to NFL front office thinking.

articles.title: Prediction markets will grow to $1 trillion by 2030, Bernstein estimates
articles.summary: Bernstein analysts predict the prediction markets industry will explode from its current size to $1 trillion by 2030. If they're right, we're looking at the mainstreaming of prediction markets from niche curiosity to legitimate financial infrastructure that could reshape how we price everything from elections to earnings.

articles.title: Polymarket Reverses Course on Startups That Piggyback on Insider Trades
articles.summary: Polymarket has changed its policy on startups that build products around insider trading activity on the platform. The reversal signals growing regulatory pressure on prediction markets and could force traders to find new ways to exploit information asymmetries.

articles.title: A casino operator wants in on a $1T U.S. event trading market
articles.summary: A major casino operator is eyeing the massive U.S. event trading market, which analysts value at $1 trillion annually. This signals that traditional gambling companies see prediction markets as the next frontier for monetizing public sentiment on everything from elections to entertainment awards.

articles.title: Kalshi Rides Legal Winning Streak Into Montana
articles.summary: Kalshi is expanding into Montana after a series of legal victories that have strengthened its position in the prediction markets space. The move signals Kalshi's growing confidence in challenging state-level restrictions on political betting, potentially opening more markets for traders nationwide.

articles.title: Prediction Markets Have an Insider Trading Problem. Are They Still Worth the Gamble?
articles.summary: Prediction markets are grappling with traders who bet on outcomes they can directly influence or have privileged information about, undermining the whole point of crowd-sourced forecasting. If insiders keep gaming the system, these markets lose their credibility as information aggregators and become just another rigged casino that smart money avoids.

articles.title: Introducing The Prediction Market Startups Database
articles.summary: We've compiled a comprehensive database tracking every startup building in the prediction markets space, from established players like Kalshi to scrappy newcomers barely out of stealth mode. This gives bettors and investors their first clear view of who's actually building the future of predictive betting and where the smart money is flowing.

articles.title: Federal judge blocks Arizona AG from prosecuting Kalshi, says it violates the Constitution
articles.summary: Arizona's attorney general tried to prosecute prediction market Kalshi, but a federal judge shut it down as unconstitutional. The ruling creates another legal precedent protecting prediction markets from state prosecutors who want to classify them as illegal gambling.

articles.title: Prediction Market Making Is Hard
articles.summary: Market makers are struggling to price prediction markets accurately, getting burned by information asymmetries and volatile swings that don't follow traditional financial patterns. This creates huge opportunities for informed bettors to exploit mispriced contracts, but also threatens the liquidity that makes these markets function in the first place.

articles.title: ‘Cowboy’: Michael Selig, a top Trump Wall Street regulator, comes out swinging
articles.summary: Michael Selig, a key Trump-era Wall Street regulator, is making aggressive moves that signal a major shift in financial oversight policy. His confrontational approach could reshape market regulations and create new volatility in financial sector stocks, making regulatory policy bets suddenly very interesting.
